children.name: ParseInput
children.purpose: Extract and validate action, repo, branch from input JSON
children.behavior: Parse JSON input, validate required fields, return structured action request
postconditions: action_request contains valid action, repo, branch

children.name: CheckBuildExists
children.purpose: Check if a build exists for given repo+branch in builds store
children.behavior: Query builds store by repo and branch, return existing build record or null

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Query builds store to check for existing build

children.signature: def CheckBuildExists(repo: str, branch: str) -> dict | None

global_vars.description: Read builds store to check existing build

data_operations.description: Query builds store by repo and branch

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns None if no build exists for repo+branch

children.name: TriggerBuild
children.purpose: Create a new build record and trigger CI/CD process
children.behavior: Generate build_id, create build record in builds store, initiate build process, return build_id and status

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Create new build record in builds store

children.signature: def TriggerBuild(repo: str, branch: str) -> dict

global_vars.description: Write new build record to builds store

data_operations.description: Create new build record with generated build_id and status 'running'

acceptance_criteria.description: New build record created with unique build_id and status 'running'

children.name: GetBuildStatus
children.purpose: Retrieve current status and logs of a build
children.behavior: Query builds store by build_id, return status and logs

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Read build record to get status and logs

global_vars.description: Read builds store to get status and logs

data_operations.description: Query builds store by build_id for status and logs

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns status and logs for valid build_id

children.name: ListBuilds
children.purpose: Return list of all builds with summary info
children.behavior: Query builds store for all records, return list of builds with id, repo, branch, status

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Read all build records to list builds

outputs.name: list_result
outputs.description: JSON with success and list of builds

data_operations.description: Scan all build records and return summary list

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of all builds with id, repo, branch, status

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Cancel a running build and update status
children.behavior: Update build record status to 'cancelled', stop build process, return confirmation

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Update build record status to cancelled

outputs.name: cancel_result
outputs.description: JSON with success and updated status

global_vars.description: Write updated status to builds store

governance_notes: All children preserve Stage1 semantics and Stage2 interfaces. ParseInput is a pure function with no resource access. Other children access the 'builds' store as specified in internal_leaf_accesses. global_vars and data_operations are synchronized: each resource access in internal_leaf_accesses appears in both global_vars and data_operations with matching op. No child accesses 'artifacts' store as per Stage1 boundary. Constraints include the one-build-per-repo+branch rule for TriggerBuild. Acceptance criteria cover each functional requirement. Node types are pure_function for ParseInput and atomic_operation for others.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All signatures remain exactly as in Stage2: ParseInput(raw_input: dict) -> dict, CheckBuildExists(repo: str, branch: str) -> dict | None, TriggerBuild(repo: str, branch: str) -> dict, GetBuildStatus(build_id: str) -> dict, ListBuilds() -> dict, CancelBuild(build_id: str) -> dict.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: internal_leaf_accesses from Stage2 are directly mapped to resources: CheckBuildExists uses builds (read), TriggerBuild uses builds (write), GetBuildStatus uses builds (read), ListBuilds uses builds (read), CancelBuild uses builds (write). ParseInput has no internal accesses.
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: For each child with internal_leaf_accesses, global_vars and data_operations both contain the same resource with the same operation type. For example, CheckBuildExists has global_vars: builds (read) and data_operations: builds (read). No mismatches.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All resources declared in Stage1 (builds, artifacts) are covered: builds is accessed by 5 children; artifacts is not accessed by any child, which is consistent with Stage1 boundary where no child requires artifacts access. The parent may use artifacts indirectly but children do not.
